title: Monitoring Activities — SOC Queue Depth vs On-Shift Analyst Headcount

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.16 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.8.16 (Monitoring activities) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.39: Monitoring Activities — SOC Queue Depth vs On-Shift Analyst Headcount.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.39' running on notable macro; soc_shift_roster.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001:2013 Annex A.16.1.2 Reporting information security events — UC evidences SOC monitoring cadence (queue depth vs shift roster) which is the operational control ensuring events are received, triaged and reported per the 2013 control.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO/IEC 27001 A.16.1.2 (Reporting information security events (2013)) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.39: Monitoring Activities — SOC Queue Depth vs On-Shift Analyst Headcount.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.39' running on notable macro; soc_shift_roster.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
search `notable` status!=closed earliest=-1d
| stats dc(rule_id) as open_notables
| append [| inputlookup soc_shift_roster.csv | where date=strftime(now(),"%Y-%m-%d") | stats sum(analysts_on_shift) as headcount]
| stats max(open_notables) as on max(headcount) as hc
| eval overload=if(on>hc*25,1,0)
description: Compares open notable or case backlog to scheduled SOC roster to evidence monitoring resourcing adequacy.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001, ISO/IEC 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Calibrate ratio to your SOC model; (2) management review; (3) hiring plan input. Lookups: `soc_shift_roster`, `soc_shift_roster.csv`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for compares open notable or case backlog to scheduled soc roster to evidence monitoring resourcing adequacy. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security, workforce CSV.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `notable` macro; `soc_shift_roster.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Calibrate ratio to your SOC model; (2) management review; (3) hiring plan input.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
search `notable` status!=closed earliest=-1d
| stats dc(rule_id) as open_notables
| append [| inputlookup soc_shift_roster.csv | where date=strftime(now(),"%Y-%m-%d") | stats sum(analysts_on_shift) as headcount]
| stats max(open_notables) as on max(headcount) as hc
| eval overload=if(on>hc*25,1,0)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Monitoring Activities — SOC Queue Depth vs On-Shift Analyst Headcount (A.8.16)** — Compares open notable or case backlog to scheduled SOC roster to evidence monitoring resourcing adequacy.

Documented **Data sources**: `notable` macro; `soc_shift_roster.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security, workforce CSV. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• Appends rows from a subsearch with `append`.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **overload** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value, Table
